methods.description: This method involves selecting sediment core sites for studying past oceanographic changes and analyzing the sediment cores for radiocarbon dating and isotopic analysis.

steps.description: We studied sediment cores VM12-107 from Bonaire Basin in the Eastern Caribbean Sea and KNR166-2-26JPC from the Florida Straits.
steps.searchable_summary: select sediment core sites for study

steps.description: Both sites host a high number of foraminifera tests that are characterized by a high level of preservation and that are sufficient for the sample demands of 14 C dating with an AMS equipped with gas ion source.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze foraminifera tests for preservation and suitability for 14 C dating

steps.description: The chronostratigraphies of both cores are constrained by AMS 14 C dating of mixed Trilobatus sacculifer/Trilobatus trilobus and G. ruber specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS 14 C dating on foraminifera specimens

steps.description: Additionally, correlate planktonic foraminiferal δ 18 O and Mg/Ca-based SST with the Greenland δ 18 O ice-core and the Hulu speleothem record.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate isotopic data with ice-core and speleothem records

steps.description: Test if systematic reductions in 14 C age differences between thermocline-and mixed layer-calcifying foraminifera during times of decline in the water-column density stratification can be observed.
steps.searchable_summary: test for systematic reductions in 14 C age differences
